channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The Hindu launches FIFA World Cup themed daily quiz featuring records and football trivia
item.description: The article refers to a daily quiz published by The Hindu focusing on the FIFA World Cup, designed to engage readers with football-related trivia and historical facts from the tournament’s rich legacy...

item.title: PCG issues safety advisory urging public to report suspected rocket debris and avoid contact
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) has issued a public advisory reminding coastal communities, particularly in areas like Cuyo, Palawan, to immediately report any suspected rocket debris while ensuring ...

item.title: Tanzania mining revenue reaches TZS 1.39 trillion in 2025/26, surpassing government target
item.description: Tanzania’s mining sector has recorded strong performance in the 2025/26 financial year, with total revenue reaching TZS 1.394 trillion (approximately USD 558 million). This result has exceeded the gov...

item.title: SERAP Demands Investigation Into N6.3bn Constituency Project Funds or Legal Action Against National Assembly Leaders
item.description: The Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project (SERAP) has called on the leadership of Nigeria’s National Assembly, specifically Senate President Godswill Akpabio and Speaker Tajudeen Abbas, to ...

item.title: Parliament moves to amend Employment Act to formally recognise remote workers in Kenya
item.description: Parliament in Kenya is considering amendments to the Employment Act to formally recognise remote workers as employees, in a move aimed at aligning the country’s labour laws with the realities of the m...

item.title: Child missing at Hamilton conservation area as kayaker dies in separate incident
item.description: Authorities in Hamilton, Ontario, are responding to two separate and serious incidents reported at or near a local conservation area. In one case, a seven-year-old child has gone missing, prompting an...

item.title: Yanga midfielder Pacôme Zouzoua begins six-week medical observation after successful surgery
item.description: Young Africans SC (Yanga) midfielder Pacôme Zouzoua has successfully undergone surgery at Aga Khan Hospital in Dar es Salaam after suffering an injury during the final match of the 2025/26 NBC Premier...

item.title: Korean National Wanted for No-Show Scam Apprehended by Authorities in Pampanga
item.description: A Korean fugitive who was wanted for an alleged no-show scam has been arrested in Pampanga province. According to reports from ABS-CBN News, the individual was the subject of an Interpol red notice, i...

item.title: Advocates urge expansion of low-income transit pass amid TransLink fare increases
item.description: Transit and poverty reduction advocates in Vancouver are calling on the provincial government to introduce a low-income transit pass, arguing that rising fares are placing an increasing financial burd...

item.title: Rescue operations underway in Kyiv after major Russian missile strike damages residential areas
item.description: Emergency rescue teams in Kyiv are continuing search and recovery efforts after a large-scale Russian missile attack struck residential areas of the Ukrainian capital. According to reporting by CNN’s ...

item.title: India’s fiscal deficit rises to 9.6% of FY target in first two months amid higher spending and lower revenues
item.description: India’s fiscal deficit stood at Rs 1.6 lakh crore in the first two months of the 2026–27 financial year (April–May), reaching 9.6% of the government’s full-year target, according to data cited by Busi...

item.title: Lagos State prosecutes over 100 offenders for illegal occupation of road medians and environmental violations
item.description: The Lagos State Government has revealed that it prosecuted more than 100 individuals within the past one year for illegally occupying road medians and breaching environmental regulations. This was dis...

item.title: Venezuelan nu-metal band Van Der Dijs members die in devastating earthquakes
item.description: All four members of Venezuelan nu-metal band Van Der Dijs have died after powerful earthquakes struck northern Venezuela on 24 June 2026. The musicians — vocalist Manuel van Der Dijs, guitarist Gabrie...

item.title: Cowboycore grooming trends to try ahead of Durban July
item.description: As anticipation builds for the Durban July, fashion and beauty trends are taking a distinctive turn towards cowboycore, driven by a global revival of Western-inspired aesthetics. The theme, often desc...

item.title: Aga Khan University Opens State-of-the-Art Research Library to Boost Academic Work
item.description: Aga Khan University (AKU) has officially opened a modern, state-of-the-art research library aimed at strengthening academic excellence and improving research capacity across its academic community. Th...

item.title: Highlights from Lusso’s New Menu Featuring Wagyu Steak Frites and River Prawn Risotto
item.description: The article takes a closer look at the refreshed offerings from Lusso, a well-known fine dining restaurant in the Philippines. Known for its elegant approach to comfort classics and European-inspired ...

item.title: Mahua Moitra alleges mob attack in Nadia, names 16 BJP workers; police deny inaction
item.description: Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra has alleged that she was targeted in a mob attack in West Bengal's Nadia district, claiming that a group of protesters hurled eggs, mud, and stones at a party office...

item.title: TCN schedules maintenance outage affecting five states and parts of Niger Republic
item.description: The Transmission Company of Nigeria (TCN) has announced a planned electricity disruption that will affect parts of Kano, Katsina, Jigawa, Bauchi, and Yobe States, as well as Gazaoua in the Niger Repub...

item.title: Attorney General defends former land registrar over irregular cancellation of War Memorial Hospital lease in Nakuru
item.description: The Office of the Attorney General has moved to defend a controversial decision involving a former Nakuru Land Registrar, Eric Nyamu, in a case concerning the War Memorial Hospital land in Milimani, N...

item.title: Major African News Roundup: Sudan Conflict Allegations, Nigeria–South Africa Dispute, Flooding in Côte d'Ivoire, Rwanda Genocide Case in Germany, and Media Restrictions in Mali
item.description: A wide-ranging African news bulletin highlights several major developments across the continent. In Sudan, Amnesty International has accused the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) of serious human rights viol...
